title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense · Code Recall
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs playtest closure, release gates, rollback, recovery, and production defense from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for production game release and defense dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.
Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Defense
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 6936 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend player value, game rules, determinism, accessibility, performance, portability, security, support, ownership, and residual risk together.

Inspect the Production Defense trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Production Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Playtest Closure has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Playtest Closure, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Playtest Closure precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Production Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Asteroids team handles case 3507 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend player value, game rules, determinism, accessibility, performance, portability, security, support, ownership, and residual risk together.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend player value, game rules, determinism, accessibility, performance, portability, security, support, ownership, and residual risk together.
